briefing.headline: Pitch it tight — summer muddy water puts a jig in its best element.
briefing.gameplan:
In summer with muddy water and pre frontal conditions, jigs presentations shine.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: When fish are stuck to the bottom in heavy current, snap a 3/4-oz football jig — not stroking it (for suspended fish) but a small snap, let it fall, snap, let it fall. This makes pressured, b
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Snapping a football jig is different from stroking — stroking is for suspended fish, snapping is a small sharp motion that makes bottom-hugging fish in heavy current react. The higher-frequency kick action of the Cleanup Craw trailer comp...

Johnston: On the Potomac, I found fish out in the grass, not in the real thick stuff. I was casting to catch them, not flipping. When that failed due to muddy water from rain, I moved to flipping grass on the Greenway Flats.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal summer bass feed aggressively. Fish split between shallow morning patterns (2-6ft) and offshore structure (12-25ft). Target grass lines, points, and ledges as falling pressure triggers movement.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
